questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A closed organ pipe resonates in its fundamental mode at a frequency of 200 Hz in $\mathrm{O}_2$ at a certain temperature. If the pipe contains 2 moles of $\mathrm{O}_2$ and, 3 moles of Ozone is now added to it, then what will be the fundamental frequency of same pipe at same temperature?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \gamma_{\text {mix }}=\frac{n_1 C_{R_1}+n_2 C_{B_1}}{n_1 C_{r_1}+n_2 C_{r_2}}=1.357, M_{\text {mix }}=\frac{2 \times 32+3 \times 48}{5}=41.6 \\ & \quad \frac{f_2}{200}=\sqrt{\frac{\gamma_{\text {mix }}}{M_{i x}} \times \frac{M}{r}}=\sqrt{\frac{1.357}{41.6} \times \frac{32}{1.4}} \\ & \therefore \quad f_2=172.7\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An equimolar mixture of a monoatomic and a diatomic ideal gas is suddenly compressed to $\frac{1}{8}$ th of its original volume. The ratio of final temperature to the initial temperature.
questions.solution: For the mixture, $\gamma=\frac{C_P^{(1)}+C_P^{(2)}}{C_V^{(1)}+C_V^{(2)}}=\frac{\frac{7}{2} R+\frac{5}{2} R}{\frac{5}{2} R+\frac{3}{2} R}=\frac{3}{2}$ Applying $\mathrm{TV}^{\gamma-1}=$ constant, the temperature increases by a factor of $\sqrt{8}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If a monoatomic gas undergoes a thermodynamic process for which its molar heat capacity is equal to the universal gas constant. What is the process in terms of V and T ?
questions.solution:
$C=R$
$\frac{d Q}{n d T}=R$
$\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{nC}_v \mathrm{dT}+\mathrm{dW}}{\mathrm{ndT}}=\mathrm{R} \Rightarrow \mathrm{Cv}+\frac{\mathrm{pdV}}{\mathrm{ndT}}=\mathrm{R} \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{pdV}}{\mathrm{ndT}}=-\frac{\mathrm{R}}{2}\left[\because \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}=3 \mathrm{R} / 2\right]$
$\Rightarrow \frac{R T}{V} \frac{d V}{d T}=-\frac{R}{2} \quad\left[\frac{P}{n}=\frac{R T}{v}\right]$
$\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{V}}=-\frac{\mathrm{dT}}{2 \mathrm{~T}} \Rightarrow \ln \left(\mathrm{VT}^{1 / 2}\right)=$ constant
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{VT}^{1 / 2}=\mathrm{constant}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
